stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of water flow rate using a flow meter. The measurements have a mean of 44.1 L/min and a standard deviation of 3.9 L/min. However, calibration tests reveal that a flow meter systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.9% and 9.4%. Estimate the true water flow rate and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 44.1 L/min. After correcting for the 7.2% bias: ≈ 47.5 L/min. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.9%-9.4%) and sampling variability: [44.7, 50.4]. Point estimate: 47.5 L/min.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
